# GeoJSON District Name Crosswalk — Article 17

**GeoJSON source:** `Ghana_New_260_District.geojson` (2017 boundary file; 260 polygons; `DISTRICT`/`REGION` props)
**Master Sheet source:** GSS Census 2021 (261 districts)
**Generated by:** `scripts/00b_build_crosswalk.py` → `data/geospatial/crosswalk_260.csv`
**Last rebuilt & verified against raw data:** 2026-06-26

---

## Coverage (hard-validated against the raw files)

- GeoJSON polygons: **260** — every one matched to exactly one Master district
- Master Sheet districts: **261**
- Matched 1:1: **260 / 260** (232 exact-normalised + 28 explicitly vetted overrides)
- Structural gap: **1** — **Guan** only (no 2017 polygon)

The generator asserts all of the above and fails loudly if any break.

---

## Correction vs the imported Project-15 crosswalk (audit finding)

The vetted P15/P16 `district_crosswalk_261_to_260.csv` was checked against **this** project's raw
`Ghana_New_260_District.geojson` and found to declare **three** structural gaps:
`Awutu Senya West`, `Sagnarigu Municipal`, `Guan`. Two of these are **false for this GeoJSON** —
the raw file actually contains the matching polygons:

| Master district | P15 said | Raw GeoJSON has | Resolved to |
|---|---|---|---|
| Awutu Senya West | gap ("absorbed in AWUTU SENYA") | `AWUTU SENYA` polygon (Central) exists | `AWUTU SENYA` |
| Sagnarigu Municipal | gap ("absorbed in TAMALE METROPOLITAN") | `SAGNERIGU` polygon (Northern) exists | `SAGNERIGU` (spelling variant) |
| Guan | gap (no polygon) | no `GUAN` polygon | **true gap** (kept) |

Result: Project 17 uses a **fully reconciled** crosswalk (260 polygons all used; Guan sole gap),
consistent with the project's own stated coverage — not the under-matched P15 version.

---

## Harmonisation method

**Step 1 — Suffix normalisation:** uppercase; drop parentheticals; remove
MUNICIPAL/METROPOLITAN/METROPOLIS/DISTRICT/ASSEMBLY/AREA/TMA; de-punctuate; collapse spaces.
→ 232 / 260 exact matches.

**Step 2 — 28 explicitly vetted overrides:** every non-exact polygon hand-checked against the raw
Master Sheet (see `OVERRIDE` dict in `00b_build_crosswalk.py`). Dangerous fuzzy look-alikes were
rejected, e.g.:

| GeoJSON | Correct master | Rejected fuzzy look-alike |
|---|---|---|
| `KASENA NANKANA EAST` | Kasena Nankana Municipal | Kasena Nankana West |
| `DORMAA MUNICIPAL` | Dormaa Central Municipal | Dormaa West / East |
| `BOLGA  EAST` | Bolgatanga East | Ga East |
| `ASSIN FOSU` | Assin Central Municipal | Assin South |
| `AWUTU SENYA` | Awutu Senya West | Awutu Senya East (taken by `AWUTU SENYA EAST`) |
| `SAGNERIGU` | Sagnarigu Municipal | Savelugu |

Amalgamated metro polygons (`ACCRA METROPOLIS`, `KUMASI METROPOLITAN`, `TEMA METROPOLITAN`,
`TAMALE METROPOLITAN`, `SEKONDI TAKORADI METROPOLIS`) map to the combined CCMA/KMA/TMA/STMA
Master entries.

---

## Guan District note

**Guan District** (Oti Region) was created by LI 2363 (2019). Population (Census 2021): **28,238**.
The 2017-boundary GeoJSON has **no Guan polygon**.

**Treatment in Article 17:**
- Included in all **tabular / ML** analyses (profiling, regression, IDRI).
- **Excluded** from choropleth maps and LISA computation (no polygon geometry).
- Declared as a study limitation in Methods and Discussion.
- **Belt zone: Southern** — Oti was carved from Volta (a Southern-belt region), consistent with the
  55-Northern / 206-Southern equity split. (Corrects an earlier note that assigned Guan to the
  Northern belt.)

---

## Usage in the spatial pipeline (Phase 4)

```python
import pandas as pd
import geopandas as gpd

gdf = gpd.read_file("../Research Datasets/Ghana_New_260_District.geojson")
xw  = pd.read_csv("data/geospatial/crosswalk_260.csv")          # built by 00b_build_crosswalk.py
gdf = gdf.merge(xw, left_on="DISTRICT", right_on="geojson_district", how="left")
# 260 polygons carry a master_sheet_district; Guan has no geometry -> excluded from maps/LISA.
```
